name: Boboli Crust
recipeIngredient: 2 small cloves of garlic, thinly sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. In a large bowl, combine flour, salt, yeast, and water. Mix well.
3. Turn dough out onto a floured surface and knead for about 15 minutes until smooth.
4. Place the dough in a lightly greased bowl, cover with a damp cloth, and let rise for 1-1/2 hours or until doubled in bulk.
5. Gently punch down the dough and turn it out onto a lightly floured board. Roll and press into a disk about 1/2 inch thick.
6. This recipe makes approximately one 12-inch pizza crust. If needed, double or reduce the recipe accordingly.
7. Rub the surface of a baking sheet with oil and place the dough on top. Make several indentations with the tip of a knife, then insert garlic slices and rosemary into the indentations.
8. Brush the surface lightly with olive oil and season with salt and pepper.
9. Bake for 10-12 minutes or until golden brown.
10. Remove from oven and carefully remove the garlic slivers. You can now top the crust, freeze it, eat it as is, or smile because you did it! Remember, if topping the crust, it's already partially baked and will only need enough time to allow the cheese and other toppings to bubble.
